
::: {.paper-table}
**Table 21. B.3 Mechanical and structural interventions**

| Intervention | Condition | Effect | Source / note |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Cataract surgery | Cataract-related blindness | Near-complete vision restoration; millions/year | Standard procedure |
| Hip replacement | End-stage hip OA | Pain elimination; function restoration; NNT~1 | Canadian and UK registry data |
| Knee replacement | End-stage knee OA | Large pain and function improvement (note: sham effect in mild OA is null) | Cf. Moseley 2002 for milder disease |
| Appendectomy | Appendicitis with perforation | Near-universal survival vs. historical ~100% mortality | Historical |
| Coronary bypass / PCI in STEMI | Acute MI, large ischemic territory | NNT ~30 for mortality; enormous for the subset with cardiogenic shock | Distinct from stable angina (see ORBITA) |
| Pacemakers | Complete heart block, severe bradycardia | Historical mortality dominant → near-normal | Standard |
| Defibrillation (VF/VT) | Shockable cardiac arrest | Survival 0% → 40–70% with early shock | AHA data |
| Tracheostomy / endotracheal intubation | Acute airway obstruction | Near-immediate rescue from certain death | Emergency medicine |
| Dialysis | ESRD | Weeks-to-live → years-to-decades | Standard |
| ECMO (neonatal respiratory failure) | Severe neonatal respiratory failure | The sole Cochrane intervention with very large mortality benefit and high-quality evidence | Pereira et al. [@pereira2012] |
| Cochlear implants | Severe-profound deafness (pre-lingual) | Functional hearing restoration; transformative | Multi-decade evidence |
:::
